more: s. m. A kind of cloth resembling gauze.

ety: Prk. लाहु and लाहओ; S. लाभः and लाभकः
def: s.m. Gain, profit, advantage.
To her.

n. m. S. लाभ Profit; gain ( prāpt ). 1. Woh to lāhā gine, na ṭoṭā . Nor gain nor loss he counts. 2. Rādhā Krishn manāë-ke jit chāhe ut jā, Lāhā hogā chaugnā, kāṅṭā lage na pā . Rādhā and Krishn invoked go where you will you 'll meet, Gains fourfold every where, no thorns shall prick your feet. 3. Jo tauṅh āṅban peḍ lagāve ho bauhtā lāhā, balmāṅ . Rus. If you plant mango trees, husband, the profits will be great.

ety: Prk. लाहु and लाहओ; S. लाभः and लाभकः

s.m. Flame (esp. without smoke); — redness; — flying or ascending (as dust).
(from لـﮩکنا) s. f. Glitter, flash.

subs: (or lahak) bolnā, To speak aloud or with confidence.

def: v. n. 1. See lahkānā , v. a. 2. To rise (as wind). lahak lahak-ke bolnā , v. n. To speak aloud or with confidence.
more: v. n. 1. To be kindled or lighted, to rise up into a flame. 2. To glitter, flash, glance, shine. 3. To wave (as, herbage before the wind). 4. To warble, to quaver.

def: n. m. The yeast or leaven from the bark of the plum, pīpal , and barrh. Sharāb kā lāhan. lāh'e, lāë , Brij. n. f. Wine.
s.m. Yeast or leaven obtained from the bark of the beṛ, pīpal, &c.
s.m. A herd; flock; pack (cf. lehaṅṛā, and leṅḍhā).
s.m. A skirt (it is tied round the loins and extends to the feet or ground).

s.m. = lahaṅḍā, q.v. s.m. A herd; flock; pack (cf. lehaṅṛā, and leṅḍhā).

s.f. A wave, billow; surge; undulation; a waving line (in patterns of cloth, &c.); a flowing or undulating fold (in draperies, &c.); — whim, fancy, vision, wild fancy, freak; — emotion, excitement, fit of passion; ecstasy, transport, rapture, frenzy; furor; a convulsive or spasmodic affection of the body (as from intoxicating or poisonous substances, from venomous bites or stings, from anger, lust, &c., or as in death); a sudden seizure, a paroxysm, fit; throe, heave; smart, ache, burn; — puff of a zephyr or soft wind; — a term for the lines which lie under the throat of certain snakes (according to the number of these lines, it is supposed, is the number of convulsive throes with which a bitten person is affected)
subs: A wave to rise; emotion, &c. to rise
A wave to come; — to prosper, to be happy
(bahar, fr. bahnā, or a jingling wood), s.f. Prosperity; prime and vigour, fulness and flush, glow and glory, zenith and heyday

v. n. To wave, to undulate.
v.n. To wave ( = lahrānā), to undulate; to rise in waves (water).

prop. n. The city of Lahor, the capital of the Panjāb.
wety: Unknown or disputed. A legend claims to be inherited from Sanskrit लव (lava, “Lava”) + पुर (pura, “fortress; city”), literally “City of Lava”, from Ramayana, a Hindu epic. Compare Punjabi لَہَوْر (lahaur).

def: adj. contrac. of lāhaurī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to Lahor; born or produced in Lahor; — a native (or a resident) of Lahor

def: s.m. = laberā, q v.
s. m. Name of a plant.

more: n. f. A hollow in which the corpse is washed. Mahad se lahad tak . Prov. From the cradle to the grave. lahad bharnā . Filling up with earth the said hollow or the grave of a deceased person, a ceremony performed on the 29th day after interment.
s. f. 1. A niche in the side of a sepulchre in which dead bodies are deposited. 2. (in Hindūstān) A place where the dead are washed.
subs: (-kī), Filling up with earth the hollow in which a corpse has been washed; filling up the grave of a deceased person (a ceremony performed on the twenty-ninth day after interment).

def: n. f. 1. A plant; Eruca sativa , Wat. 2. A fine silk cloth.

part. act. (of لحق) Reaching, overtaking, adhering, adjoining, pertaining. adj. Adjoined; (substantively) An appendage.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لاحق, from Arabic لَاحِق (lāḥiq).

v. a. 1. See بـﮩکانا (7 and 8). 2. To make a flame or blaze. 3. To tallyho.
more: (causal of لـﮩکنا) v. a. 1. To warble, to quaver. 2. To cause to shine or glitter. 3. To blow up a fire.

v. a. To fondle, to pat and encourage a horse, &c.
E. n. m. Rice and milk, etc. presented to the bride and bridegroom. Biyāh kā ashgun mālūm bhaë, lahkor meṅ āë bhaṭṭā . Prov. Plain is the marriage's ill portent when rubbish is for present sent.
more: s. m. Rice-milk eaten by bride and bridegroom.

s.f. Glittering, flashing; glitter, flash; blaze.
s. f. Glittering, flashing.
adj. Glittering.
adj. (f. -ī), Shining, bright, glittering, resplendent.
adj. (f. -ī), Waving, undulating, bending (as corn, &c. before the wind); — flourishing, blooming, luxuriant.
adj. 1. Waving; undulating. 2. Blooming; flourishing; luxuriant. Mahal meṅ ajāib hue chahchahe, Voh murjhāë gul phir hue lahlahe . Mīr Hasan.
s.f. Waving or bending (of corn, &c. before the wind).

v. n. 1. To wave (a field). 2. To bloom; to be verdant; to flourish. Ilāhī! tū lahlahā'tā jāë! A curse. Mah. Wom. God grant you may die in your youth! lahlahāhaṭ' ; Tir. lahlahā , n. f. The waving of a corn field, etc. Haiṅ is harā meṅ kyā kyā barsāt kī bahāreṅ, Sabzoṅ kī lahlahāhaṭ, bāgāt kī bahāreṅ! Nazīr.
more: v. n. To bloom, to be verdant, to flourish.

s.f. Stiff or thick mud.
adj. Borrowing and never paying.
adj. & s.m. Borrowing and never paying; — one who borrows and never pays.
s.m. Flesh, meat (syn. gosht, māṅs); — solder.
more: (Heb. ) 1. s. m. Flesh, meat. 2. Solder. 3. ¶ laḥim, adj. Carnivorous.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَحْم (lahm), from Arabic لَحْم (laḥm).

def: s.m. corr. of لمحه lamḥa, q.v. s.m. A glance of the eye; twinkling of an eye; a minute, a moment

s.f. corr. of لکشمي lakshmī, q.v. s.f. Good fortune, good-luck, fortune, prosperity; wealth, riches; success, happiness; beauty, grace, loveliness, charm, splendour; — royal power, dominion; superhuman power; — a pearl; — name of the goddess of fortune and beauty (she is regarded as the wife of Vishṇu or Nārāyaṇ, and is one of the principal female deities of the Hindūs; she is also called Padmā, Kamalā and Śrī, and is supposed to be the Ceres of the ancients); — an epithet of Sītā, the wife of Rāma
subs: s.m. 'The husband or lord of Lakshmī or Fortune,' a name of Vishṇu; lord or possessor of fortune or wealth, a wealthy man

s.f. 'The worship of Lakshmī,' name of a festival on the fifteenth day in the dark half of the month Kārtik, Sept.-Oct. (said to be celebrated, in modern times, by bankers and traders, to propitiate Fortune); — worship of Lakshmī by the bridegroom and bride after the bride has been brought to her father-in-law's house
s.f. 'Shadow (or protection or favour) of Lakshmī'; good fortune, prosperity, success
(kisī-ke) ghar-meṅ ānā or utarnā, 'Fortune to come to the house' (of); to enjoy good fortune or prosperity, to prosper, thrive; to have a run of good luck
(-ko), To offer (a meal, &c.) to Lakshmī and Nārāyaṇ before commencing (to eat, &c.); to begin to eat (syn. bismillāh karnā)
adj. (f. -vatī), Fortunate, prosperous, thriving; wealthy; handsome, beautiful.

def: s.f. Sound, voice; note, tone; modulation, melody.
more: s. f. 1. A sound, a note. 2. Melody, modulation.

ety: lah˚ = Prk. लह(इ) = लभ(ते), rt. लभ्
def: v.t. To find, get, experience; — v.n. To get on well, to prosper, flourish; — to accrue; to avail, answer, boot, signify; — s.m. Profit, gain; outstanding debt; — lot, portion, fortune, fate, destiny

n. m. S. लहंडा 1. Profit; gain. 2. Fortune; fate (قسمت 4). 1. Jiske lahne kā hogā use milegā . 2. Āṅkheṅ khol khol-ke dekhe le le-ke siskī, Lūṭ khasoṭ le gaë saṅghātī, lahne kī jiskī . Kabīr. Opens his eyes the dying man and seeing sighs, What fate assigns each friend bears off e'en 'fore he dies. lah'nā, lahnā pāonā , adj. Lucky; auspicious.
more: 1. v. a. To find, to get, to experience. 2. v. n. To avail, to answer, to boot, to signify, to accrue. 3. s. m. Outstanding debt. 4. Fate, destiny.

def: Bhoj. n. m.A herd of buffalos.
n. m. S. लहू A skirt. lah'ṅgā pahannā , v. n. 1. To put on a skirt. 2. To become a woman (said to a man). Lahṅgā pahan lo!
more: s. m. A petticoat (or a mere skirt, which is tied round the loins and extends to the feet or ground. Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: n. f. S. लहरी 1. A wave; billows; surge. 2. An undulation; see-saw. 3. Emotion; excitement; fit of passion. 1. Kāle kī sī ek lahar ā jātī hai . 2. Jal gayā jaṅgal, jal gaī būṭī, ādhī rāt lahar mere chhūṭī, Dārū nahīṅ miltī, hakīm nāhīṅ miltā, aisī re kismat phūṭī! Kahāṅ se lāūṅ, darad, terī būṭī? Double entendre. Wom. Song. 5. Extasy; transport; rapture. 6. A paroxysm; fit. 7. Smart; ache; burn. 8. Frenzy; heat; furor. lahr uṭhnā , v. n. To arise (emotion, passion, extasy). lahr ānā , v. n. To prosper; to be happy. Khūb lahr ā rahī hai . lahr-chāl ; A harkat-i-maujiyah ; S. turaṅg gati , n. f. ( Mech .) Undulatory motion. lahr chaṛhnā , v. n. To have a fit or paroxysm. lahr-dār, lahrī , ( Bot .) Repand. lahr kā patlā hissa , ( Acous .) Rarefication of a portion of the wave. lahr kā gāṛhā ṭukṛā, yā hissā . ( Acous .) Condensation of a portion of the wave.
more: s. f. 1. A wave, undulation. 2. Whim, fancy, vision. 3. The effect of the poison of a snake. 4. A waving pattern in embroidery. 5. Emotion.

def: s.m. A quaver, shake, trill (in music); — a trilling lay, a lively air or tune; — (dialec.) a skirt (syn. lahangā)
subs: or lahrā bajānā, To play a lively tune; — to give (one) a shoe-beating
To raise visions or hopes (of, -kā, a thing, in, -meṅ, a person), to excite, to tantalize; — to play fast and loose (with); to put off, evade; to avoid performing an agreement.

def: n. m. 1. A lively tune. 2. A quaver; shake; trill. lahrā uṛānā, yā bajānā , v. a. 1. To play a lively tune. 2. To shoe-beat. lahrā lagānā , E. v. a. To excite (اُکسانا 4, 6).
more: Bund. n. m. A kind of grain ( bājrā 1).
s. m. Quaver, shake, trill (in musick).
s. m. A younger, junior, cadet.
s. m. Name of a grain. (باجرا bājrā, Holcus spicatus).

def: To evade, to avoid performing an agreement.

n. m. Mantle and skirt. Chhoṛī dehū, gorī, lahrā paṭorvā, cholī ke band dehū phāṛi! Bāṅdhi lehu, gorī, sirvā meṅ pagiyā! ehī mor hulās . Bhoj. Song. Take off, fair one, your wrap and skirt, your boddice take off too, A turban on your head put on, the advice I give to you.
v. n. 1. To wave; undulate. Khet khūb lahrā rahā hai . 2. To blaze up.
more: (from लहरी see لـﮩر lahar ) v. n. 1. To tantalize. 2. To undulate, fluctuate, to wave. 3. To play loosely, to put off.

def: n. f. Prosperity; happiness. Ek āṅkh meṅ lahar bahar, ek āṅkh meṅ Khudā kā qahr . Prov. One eye doth prosperous shine, the other cursed with wrath Divine. (A one-eyed man).

adj. & s.m. Fanciful, whimsical, capricious, fitful, inconstant, fickle; emotional; — merry, gay, jovial; — odd, fantastic; — careless, inconsiderate, thoughtless; — a fanciful (or an emotional) person, &c.; a merry-andrew, a clown.

more: v. n. To be abashed or ashamed; to take shame to oneself; to blush. 1. Nāī sab ke pāoṅ dhoë, apne pāoṅ dhote lajāë . Prov. The barber washes every body's feet, to wash his own he is ashamed. 2. Lajāyal larikā dhiṅḍhkī ṭove . Bhoj. Prov. The shy child feels his stomach. 3. 'Suno, bābā, tum hameṅ lajāvan āë, Āj hamāre sāmagrī, kyā sāmāṅ lāë?' Bābā kahe 'suno beṭī! taine bodī bāt bichārī hai, Ek palak meṅ sab kuchh lāve, hamārā Sāṅval sāh Girdhārī hai .' Narsī jī kā bhāt. 'Would you, my father, 'fore them all bring me to shame? What have you brought with you? today they 'll presents claim.' 'Daughter! these are unworthy thoughts, whate'er betide My god Kanhaya quickly will all things provide.'
v. a. To put to shame. Jin jāī unheṅ lajāī . Prov. She shames the mother who gave her birth. lajāl'ū , adj. Modest; bashful. lajā'ū , adj. Shameful; ignominious.

def: adj. S. लक्ष A hundred thousand; a lac. 1. Use ek pān lākh lākh banāo detā hai . Wom. One pān makes her a lac lac times more beautiful. 2. Lākh kulfat ko dekhte haiṅ ham, Dugnā ulfat ko dekhte haiṅ ham . Though sorrows hundred thousand meet my eye Yet twice as much affection I descry. 3. Lākhoṅ meṅ ek hai . He is one in a million. lākh, kitnā hī , adv. Any number; heaps; any extent. lākh jī se , adv. With all one's heart. Koī lākh jī se ho mujh par fidā, Maiṅ tujh par fidā hūṅ, mujhe is se kyā ? Mīr Hasan. What though a lak times be a heart devote to me, What's it to me? my heart is all devote to thee. lākh sir kā h. , v. n. 1. To insist; persist. 2. To turn a deaf ear to. lākh kā ghar khāk h. , v. n. To be reduced to poverty. lākh man kā h. , v. n. 1. To be very heavy or immovable. 2. To be a man of weight or consideration. lākhoṅ meṅ , adv. 1. Before every body; in public. 2. Certainly; assuredly. Woh lākhoṅ meṅ āëgā. lakh-luṭ , adj. Extravagant; prodigal.
more: n. m. S. लाक्षा. 1. Gum lac. 2. Sealing wax. Lākh ṭake kī ser, paise kī kitnī ? A pun. lākh kā chūṛā , n. m. Bangles made of lac. Chūṛā hai lākh kā! Street cry. lākh kī battī , n. f. A stick of sealing wax. chapṛā lākh . Shell lac.

def: n. f. 1. Wood. S. लकुट 2. A staff; stick. 1. Gīlī lakṛī sīdhī ho saktī hai . Prov. Damp wood may be made straight. (A child may be trained). 2. Hāth kī lakaṛyā līnī, kāṅdhe ke kamalyā līnī, sādhū ko dukh de gayo re . Sūr Dās. His staff from him he took, his blanket took, the holy man he hath afflicted sore. lakṛī , adj. 1. Stift; rigid. 2. Emaciated; lean. lakṛī pheṅknā , Cudgel-playing ( paṭā-bāzī ). lakṛī-khānā . ( Mech .) A block. ek lakṛī sab ko hāṅknā , v. a. lit . to drive every one with the same stick. To fail in showing the consideration due to each one's position. sūkh-ke lakṛī h. , v. n. To grow lean or emaciated. lakṛiyāṅ denā , v. a. To place wood on the funeral pyre. Maiṅ tumhāre sāmne mar-jāūṅ, aur tum mujhe lakaṛiyāṅ do!

s.f. The red animal dye called 'lac'; — the insect which produces lac (it is analogous to the cochineal insect, and, like it, forms, when dried and prepared, a dye of a red colour; its nest is formed of a resinous substance which is used as sealing-wax, and is called shell-lac, vulg. shellac)
subs: s.m. The red Lodh-tree (from the bark of which an astringent infusion is prepared which is used to fix colour in dyeing)
(for lākshā-gr̤ih), s.m. Name of an ancient palace near Benares
s.m. The tree Butea frondosa (syn. palās).

def: s.m. One hundred thousand, a lac; — a mark, butt; object of aim; — disguise, fraud
subs: (˚sha+av˚), adv. Up to a hundred thousand.

def: s.m. A mark, spot; distinctive mark, sign, symptom, token, indication, characteristic; feature; character, attribute, quality; destiny; — accurate description, definition; a predicate; designation, appellation, name; — the Indian crane.
s.f. Indirect indication or hint; metonymy; metaphorical sense (as distinguished from the literal); the female of the Indian crane; a goose.

s.f. (dialec.) = lakshmī, q.v. s.f. Good fortune, good-luck, fortune, prosperity; wealth, riches; success, happiness; beauty, grace, loveliness, charm, splendour; — royal power, dominion; superhuman power; — a pearl; — name of the goddess of fortune and beauty (she is regarded as the wife of Vishṇu or Nārāyaṇ, and is one of the principal female deities of the Hindūs; she is also called Padmā, Kamalā and Śrī, and is supposed to be the Ceres of the ancients); — an epithet of Sītā, the wife of Rāma
subs: s.m. 'The husband or lord of Lakshmī or Fortune,' a name of Vishṇu; lord or possessor of fortune or wealth, a wealthy man

s.f. 'The worship of Lakshmī,' name of a festival on the fifteenth day in the dark half of the month Kārtik, Sept.-Oct. (said to be celebrated, in modern times, by bankers and traders, to propitiate Fortune); — worship of Lakshmī by the bridegroom and bride after the bride has been brought to her father-in-law's house
s.f. 'Shadow (or protection or favour) of Lakshmī'; good fortune, prosperity, success
(kisī-ke) ghar-meṅ ānā or utarnā, 'Fortune to come to the house' (of); to enjoy good fortune or prosperity, to prosper, thrive; to have a run of good luck
(-ko), To offer (a meal, &c.) to Lakshmī and Nārāyaṇ before commencing (to eat, &c.); to begin to eat (syn. bismillāh karnā)
adj. (f. -vatī), Fortunate, prosperous, thriving; wealthy; handsome, beautiful.

def: part. (f. -ā), Beheld, looked upon; seen, perceived, observed; discerned, discovered; known (from signs, indications, or appearances); — marked.

s.m. Name of a son of Daśaratha by his wife Su-mitrā (he was the younger half-brother and faithful companion of Rāmćandra during his travels and adventures); a proper name; — the Indian crane, Ardea sibirica; — a mark, sign, token, spot ( = lakshaṇ); — a name; — adj. (f. -ā), Possessed of lucky signs or marks, lucky, fortunate, prosperous.
s.f. The female of the Indian crane; — name of a wife of Kr̤ishṇa and mother of Śāmba; a proper name.

s.f. Good fortune, good-luck, fortune, prosperity; wealth, riches; success, happiness; beauty, grace, loveliness, charm, splendour; — royal power, dominion; superhuman power; — a pearl; — name of the goddess of fortune and beauty (she is regarded as the wife of Vishṇu or Nārāyaṇ, and is one of the principal female deities of the Hindūs; she is also called Padmā, Kamalā and Śrī, and is supposed to be the Ceres of the ancients); — an epithet of Sītā, the wife of Rāma
more: n. f. Pālī, lakkhī ; Pr. lachchhī . 1. The wife of Vishnu, the goddess of wealth and prosperity. She is also called Padma, Kāmdā, and Srī, and is supposed to be the Ceres of the Romans. 2. Wealth; prosperity. 3. Beauty; splendour. 4. A polite term for the daughter-in-law ( bahū , 1). lakshmī pūjā , n. 1. The Hin. worship of Lakshmī on the last day of the dark fortnight of Kārtik (Sept.—Oct.). 2. A ceremony in honor of the same, observed by a bride and bridegroom when the bride has been brought to the house of her husband. lakshmī ghar meṅ ānā , v. n. 1. To prosper; thrive. 2. To have a run of good luck. lakshmī nārāin k. , v. a. 1. To offer one's meal to Lakshmī and Nārāin before partaking of it. 2. To begin to eat.
subs: s.m. 'The husband or lord of Lakshmī or Fortune,' a name of Vishṇu; lord or possessor of fortune or wealth, a wealthy man

s.f. 'The worship of Lakshmī,' name of a festival on the fifteenth day in the dark half of the month Kārtik, Sept.-Oct. (said to be celebrated, in modern times, by bankers and traders, to propitiate Fortune); — worship of Lakshmī by the bridegroom and bride after the bride has been brought to her father-in-law's house
s.f. 'Shadow (or protection or favour) of Lakshmī'; good fortune, prosperity, success
(kisī-ke) ghar-meṅ ānā or utarnā, 'Fortune to come to the house' (of); to enjoy good fortune or prosperity, to prosper, thrive; to have a run of good luck
(-ko), To offer (a meal, &c.) to Lakshmī and Nārāyaṇ before commencing (to eat, &c.); to begin to eat (syn. bismillāh karnā)
adj. (f. -vatī), Fortunate, prosperous, thriving; wealthy; handsome, beautiful.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit लक्ष्मी (lakṣmī).

def: adj. & s.m. To be marked; to be seen or noted; observable, perceptible, visible; recognizable; — an aim, object (to be aimed at), butt, target; — mere appearance, sham, pretence, fraud, disguise; — a lac, a hundred thousand.
s.m. Lac (the animal dye); — a rag, &c. ( = lattā, q.v.). s.m. A dirty and tattered cloth; a scrap of cloth, a rag, tatter; clothes (as in kapṛā-lattā).
s.f. corr. of lagtī, q.v. s.f. Attachment, affection, love, &c. (i.q. lāg, lagan, and lagī, qq.v.).
more: n. f. 1. A poker; brand. 2. A tell-tale. 3. A mischief-maker; fire-brand.

def: s.m. Stick, staff, cudgel, club, bludgeon.
more: s. m. A staff, a stick, club.

ety: lakuṭ, q.v. + S. इका; Prk. इआ
def: s.f. A small club or bludgeon (especially the club held by Krishṇa).
s.m. Spasmodic distortion of the face or mouth; — paralysis
more: n. m. 1. W. Distortion of the face. 2. E. Paralysis. laqvah mār jānā, yā h. , v. n. To have paralysis; to be paralysed. Aisā lappaṛ dūṅgā ki laqve kā āzār ho jāëgā . Slang.
(from لقا) s. m. A spasmodick distortion of the face.
subs: (-ko), or laqwa ho ā (-ko), To be stricken with paralysis, to have a paralytic stroke, to be paralyzed.

ety: from the Persian; prob. akin to S. रक्त
def: adj. Red (syn. surḵẖ), ruddy; red-hot; inflamed; enraged; — s.m. A ruby (cf. laʻl); the male of the bird Fringilla amandava (cf. muniyā); — the seed of Abrus precatorius (syn. ghuṅgćī)
more: adj. S. लल wish for. Emph. lāl lāl . See surkh . 1. Red. 1. Dekhā shahr Baṅgālā, dāṅt lāl, muṅh kālā . Prov. The Bengal women I have met, red teeth all show in black mouths set. 2. Gāliyāṅ degā to daspanā lāl kar-ke zabān par rakh dūṅgī . Mah. Wom. If you give me abuse I'll burn your tongue with red hot pincers. 2. Ruddy; florid. 3. Inflamed. 4. Enraged. 5. Dear; darling. Lāloṅ kā lāl ban rahā hai. lāl , n. m. 1. An infant; a son. Lāl piyārā to uskā khayāl bhī piyārā . Prov. Love the child, love its ways. 2. The amadavat; Estrelda amandava . 3. (wrongly written لعل) A Hin. surname. lāl aṅgārā, lāl surkh , adj. 1. Red hot. 2. Very red. 3. Flushed with rage; fiery. Iske sunte hī gazab ho-ke woh lāl aṅgārā, Lāṭhī pāṭhī jo na pāī to phir jhunjhlā, Kheṅch mārā mere sīne pe uṭhā-kar tarbūz . Nazīr. On hearing this as red hot coals she grew, No stick at hand, with rage she at me flew, And full …
1. adj. Red. لال ساگ lāl sāg, s. m. Name of a vegetable. (Amaranthus gangeticus. Willd. ). 2. Dumb. 3. s. m. A ruby. 4. adj. Inflamed.

1. (r. लल Wish for, or लड Sport) adj. Darling, dear; s. m. An infant boy, a son. 2. ( लाला ) s. m. Spittle, saliva. 3. s. m. The name of a bird. (The male of Fringilla amandava). لال بجهکڙ lāl bujhakkaṛ, An intelligent person; one who comprehends quickly, or who readily divines what has happened or is to happen from certain symptoms; but, the term is generally used ironically for a wiseacre, an ignorant foolish person who pretends to more sense than his neighbours; as, in the following answer given by a person of this description, when consulted respecting the marks of an elephantʼs feet in mire, by people who had never seen such an animal: يـﮩ توبوجهي لال بجهکڙ اورنه بوجهي کوي پاين چکي باندهکرمت هرنا کوداهوي Yih to būjhe lāl bujhakkaṛ aur na būjhe koʼe; pāyan chakkī bāndhkar mat harnā kūdā hoʼe. Lāl Bujhakkaṛ and none else can understand this; may not an antelope with a millstone tied t…
subs: or lāl-surḵẖ, or lāl-lāl, adj. Very red; red-hot; fiery, flushed with passion
adj. Flaming red; fiercely or vividly red
v.n. To become (or turn) red, to redden; to become red-hot; to flush; to get into a rage
'To show red and yellow eyes'; to glare fiercely; to fly into a passion
or lāl ćitr-mol, s.m. Plumbago rosea
v.n. To flourish, to be prosperous, to be happy (e.g. lāl raho, 'Continue flourishing! be prosperous!')
s.m. Red amaranth (used as a vegetable), Amaranthus paniculatus, or A. gangeticus
or lāl kaddū, s.m. The red pumpion, Curcurbita maxima
s.f. The red book; a book to which an ignoramus refers for the solution of every difficulty; — a book of reference; — lāl kurtī, s.f. A red coat; — the European infantry lines in an Indian cantonment
A regiment of red-coats, i.e. of British infantry
s.f. Red oleander, or rose-bay, Nerium odorum
s.f. Red pepper; cayenne; red chilli, capsicum
v.n. To become red, &c. ( = lāl paṛnā, q.v.); — to be on the point of winning (at play); to be 'hot,' or on the point of discovering anything (as a hidden handkerchief, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَال (lāl).

ety: Pers. also lāl; prob. S. लाल, fr. caus. of rt. लल्; cf. lāṛ and lāṛlā
def: adj. Beloved, darling, dear, precious; — dumb; — s.m. An infant boy, a son; a darling, a pet; — a proper name (among Hindūs)
subs: s.m. An ignorant (or a stupid) fellow who pretends to knowledge or acuteness, an ignoramus, a jackanapes, wiseacre
or lāl-gurū, s.m. The priest of the sweeper caste
adj. & s.m. Most dear; very dear; — the dearest of dear ones.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَال (lāl).

def: s.m. (f.?) = lār, and lālā, 'spittle,' q.v. s.m.(f.?) Saliva, spittle, slaver, dribbling, dribble of an infant (syn. rāl).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَال (lāl).

def: s. A kind of pumpion.
s.m. A ruby; — any bright gem; (met.) the lips (of a mistress)
more: n. m. A ruby. Gūdaṛ meṅ lāl nahīṅ chhuptā . Prov. A ruby is not hid in rubbish? lāl ugalnā , v. n. 1. ( Ironic .) To cast up rubies. Aise hī woh lāl ugalte haiṅ jo unkī khushāmad karūṅ! 2. To abuse. Bas ab ziyādah lāl na uglo! lāl-lab , Poet. adj. Red-lipped.
subs: v.n. 'To cast up rubies'; (ironic.) to utter (or pour forth) abusive or scurrilous words (e.g. bas, ab ziyāda laʻl na uglo)

def: s.f. Saliva, spittle, slaver, mucous
more: s. m. Sir, master, a schoolmaster.

s. m. 1. A slave, an upper servant. 2. Talkativeness or frivolous talk. 3. An herb brought from about Mecca, the smell of which keeps off the piles. 4. adj. Brilliant (a pearl). 5. s. A grain like sesamum. 6. For لاله lāla, q. v.
subs: s.m. (lit. 'having saliva for food'), Name of the hell appropriated to those who eat their meals without first offering food to the gods, to the manes, or to guests
s.m. Passing mucous urine; mucous urine.

ety: from the Pers.; prob. akin to S. लाल, fr. the caus. of rt. लल्
def: s.m. Sir, master; a school-master; a grandee; a respectable Hindū (as a banker, &c.); the designation of a class of Kāyaths who are schoolmasters; a writer, clerk, copyist; a title given to persons of the Vaiśya class, &c.; a respectful term of address to a father, or a father-in-law; (for lallā or lalā, q.v.) dear boy, darling; — (in Persian) the chief (or an upper) servant (intrusted with the education of his master's sons); a major domo; — a slave
subs: a babbler; — babbling, prating, talkativeness, frivolous talk; — a kind of grain resembling sesame; — a kind of herb brought from the neighbourhood of Mecca (the smell of which is said to keep off the piles)
s.m. An affectionate (or a respectful) form of address (commonly applied to Banyās)
(-ko), To sing or pat to sleep, to soothe or coax to sleep
s.m. A form of address (used towards a respectable man not of high rank); Master, Sir; father, father-in-law.

def: adj. Bright, shining, resplendent (pearls).
s.m. = لالا lālā, q.v. s.f. Saliva, spittle, slaver, mucous
subs: s.m. (lit. 'having saliva for food'), Name of the hell appropriated to those who eat their meals without first offering food to the gods, to the manes, or to guests
s.m. Passing mucous urine; mucous urine.

def: s.m. A tulip; (in India, also) the red poppy; — a glass shade
more: n. m. 1. A tulip. 2. In India, the poppy flower. 3. A glass shade. lālah-rukh, yā uzār , Poet. adj. Tulip-cheeked; rosy-cheeked. lālah-zār , n. A bed or garden of tulips. lālā-gūṅ, lālā-fām, lālā-raṅg , Red.

n. m. 1. A respectable Hindu, as a banker, etc. Lālā jī , Sir or Master. 2. ( lālā jī , Hin.) Father; father-in-law. 3. ( Rus .) A darling (boy). lālā bhāī . Banyās so called. lālā bhaiyā k . To caress; show affection. lālā bhaiyā kar-ke sulānā , v. n. To sing or pat to sleep. lālā jī , n. A honorific form of address. lālī , n. f. A dear girl.

s. m. A tulip. لاله رخ lāla-ruḵẖ or لاله رخسار lāla-ruḵẖsār or لاله عذار lāla-iﻌẕār, adj. Tulip-cheeked, red-cheeked. لاله رنگ lāla-rang, adj. Tulip-coloured. لاله زار lāla-zār or لاله ستان lāla-sitān, A bed or garden of tulips.
subs: or lāla-ruḵẖsār, or lāla-ʻiẕār, adj. Tulip-cheeked, red-cheeked
s.m. A bed or garden of tulips

def: s.m. corr. of P. لاله lāla, q.v.

ety: Prk. ललओ, or लल्लओ?; S. लड or लल+कः
def: s.m. A boy; dear boy, darling; — a dolt, blockhead, fool ( = S. laṭa); — adj. (f. -ī), Impotent
more: n. m. 1. A term of endearment. 2. A boy. 3. ( lalā ) A green horn (احمق). 1. Mere lalā kī ulṭī rīt, Sāvan mās uṭhāveṅ bhīt . Prov. My son must needs the practice of the world amend, My simple builds his wall when Sāvan rains descend. 2. Ghuṅgṭā! terī oṭ karan nā pāī do batiyāṅ, Lalnā ko maiṅ palnā meṅ ḍāl āī, chalī āī tere sāth . Rus. Wom. Song. O veil that wraps my face! through thee two words I could not say, My babe I've in his cradle laid and come with you away. 3. Chhallā de to lallā pāve, Paisā de to god khilāve . Hījrās. Eunuchs. A ring give me, a son shall be your meed, Pice give, a child you shall in your lap feed! badāūṅ ke lalā , n. m. An arrant fool of Badāun.
subs: A Badāʼuṅ fool; a great dolt or blockhead (cf. the Eng. 'Essex calf').

def: s.m. Longing, eager desire; covetousness, cupidity, greed, avarice; selfishness; occasion; view; want; — incentive to cupidity, a bait, lure
more: n. m. S. लालसा 1. Covetousness (طمع 1). 1. Lālach gun ghar binās . Prov. Greed destroys every good quality. 2. Lālach bas partok nasāë . Prov. In toils of greed Heaven lost. 3. Makkhī baiṭhī shahad par aur pankh gaë lipṭāë, Hāth male aur sir dhune, lālach burī balāë . Dohā. A fly upon the honey lights and in 't his wings are stuck, He rubs his hands and beats his head, greed brings such evil luck! lālach denā , v. a. To allure; entice; decoy. lālach k. , v. a. To be greedy, avaricious. lālach meṅ ānā , v. n. To take the bait.
s. m. Longing, covetousness, greediness, selfishness, avarice, occasion, view, want. لالچ کرنا lālach karnā, To covet, to long for.
subs: (-ko), To appeal to the cupidity of, to offer a bait (to), to tempt, allure. entice, decoy
(-kā), To be greedy (of or after), to covet; to practise avarice, to be avaricious
(-ke), To be moved by desire or greed (for), to take a bait.

ety: Prk. लल्लक्क; prob. fr. S. लल+कृ
def: s.f. A sudden gush (of any liquid); — eager desire, longing, hankering, craving; — whim, fancy, caprice; — zeal, ardour, enthusiasm.
more: n. f. Cont . of lallo tongue, lapak bound. 1. A sudden gush of water, etc. 2. Longing; hankering. 3. Zeal; ardour; enthusiasm. 4. ( Met .) Whim; fancy; caprice.
s. f. 1. A sudden gush of water, &c. 2. ( met. ) Whim, fancy, caprice.

ety: fr. the caus. of rt. lal
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Caressing, fondling, coaxing, wheedling, seducing, seductive; — a coaxer, wheedler, seducer
subs: s.f. A seductive woman, a wanton.

def: v.n. To be possessed with a longing (for), to long or crave (for); to be greedy (for), to covet; — v.t. To attack, to fall upon.
more: v. n. H. lallo tongue. To long for; be greedy for; die for. Jahāṅ koī chīz dekhī lhilak paṛā .

def: s.m. A mark or spot on the forehead; — a mark, spot; — an ornament for the forehead; — ornament, decoration, embellishment; — a banner, flag; — a line, row; — a mane; tail.
s.m. 1˚ — lalām, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of nīlām, q.v. s.m. An auction, a public sale; — adv. By auction; at auction
subs: s.m. An auction-purchaser; one who holds an estate, &c. purchased at a public sale
or kar-denā, or nīlān karānā, v.t. To sell by auction
v.t. To send to an auction
v.n. To be sold by public auction.

def: s.m. A chaplet or wreath of flowers worn on the forehead.
s.m. = lalām, q.v. s.m. A mark or spot on the forehead; — a mark, spot; — an ornament for the forehead; — ornament, decoration, embellishment; — a banner, flag; — a line, row; — a mane; tail.
s.f. A kind of ear-ornament.
adj. Caressing, fondling, indulging, spoiling; coaxing, wheedling; — s.m. The act of caressing, fondling, petting, &c. &c.; — s.f. A mistress, sweetheart
s.m. Play, sport, pastime; pleasure; dalliance; — the playing or moving to and fro of the tongue; lolling the tongue; — the tree Shorea robusta.
s.f. A woman, a wife; — a playful woman; a woman of pleasure, a wanton woman.
s.m. A boy ( = lalā).

s.m. = lalāṭ, q.v. s.m. Forehead, brow; — (met.) fate, destiny, fortune (conceived, by the Hindūs, to be written on the forehead); — lalāṭ-rekhā, s.f. A line on the forehead (supposed to indicate long life); a wrinkled or corrugated brow; — a coloured sectarial mark (worn by Hindūs) on the forehead.
s.f. Longing, ardent desire, eager longing for an absent person or object; covetousness (see lālać); the longing of a pregnant woman; regret, sorrow; missing; asking (for), soliciting; dalliance.
adj. Very desirous or wishful.

s.m. Forehead, brow; — (met.) fate, destiny, fortune (conceived, by the Hindūs, to be written on the forehead); — lalāṭ-rekhā, s.f. A line on the forehead (supposed to indicate long life); a wrinkled or corrugated brow; — a coloured sectarial mark (worn by Hindūs) on the forehead.
more: s. m. 1. The forehead. 2. Fate, destiny, fortune.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ललाट (lalāṭa).

def: adj. Relating to the forehead; being on the forehead; relating to fate, arising from destiny; — looking attentively at the forehead (of a master), attentive; — s.m. An attentive servant; — a careless person; an idler.
s.f. An ornament worn on the forehead; a mark made with sandal on the forehead.
s.m. (rustic) A boy, &c. ( = lalā, q.v.). s.f. Saliva, spittle, slaver, mucous
subs: s.m. (lit. 'having saliva for food'), Name of the hell appropriated to those who eat their meals without first offering food to the gods, to the manes, or to guests
s.m. Passing mucous urine; mucous urine.

ety: lālać, q.v. + ā˚ = āw = Prk. आवे or आव = S. आपि;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be possessed with a longing (for, -par), to long (for), hanker (after), lust (for); to be tantalized; — v.t. To excite desire in; to rouse the cupidity of; to tantalize.
more: v. n. S. लल desire. 1. To long for; hanker after; water (the mouth); lust for. lalchān'ā , v. a. To excite desire; tantalize.
v. n. To be tantalized, to long. v. a. To excite desire, to tantalize, to covet.

def: part. adj. Desired, longed for, coveted.
n. m. Plumbago rosea . Wat.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Covetous, cupidinous, greedy, grasping, a varicious, miserly, niggardly, selfish, interested; — a grasping or selfish man, &c.
more: adj. Avaricious; covetous. 1. Lālchī ko jahān taṅg . Prov. For the avaricious man the world's too small. 2. Lobhī gurū, lālchī chelā, narak kunḍ meṅ ṭhelam ṭhelā . A covetous priest, and a greedy disciple in Hell's black waters hustle.
